title: Climber v3 — converging on a winch that won't skip teeth
summary: Iterated on the climber spool + ratchet. Two failure modes identified on the practice field, both fixed before competition.
body:
Spent the night on the practice field trying to reproduce the climb failure we saw at the district event.

**Findings:**

* The Dyneema was jumping out of the spool groove whenever the robot was pulled at an angle > 15°.
* The one-way bearing was occasionally back-driving under high preload because the set screw had loosened.

**Fixes landing tomorrow:**

1. Swapping to a deeper V-groove spool (printed in PA-CF for tonight, machined aluminum for Champs).
2. Adding a second set screw on a flat I milled into the bearing inner race.
3. Dialing the preload so the ratchet engages within ~5° of rotation.

Going to test on the practice field tomorrow with a worst-case 25° pull angle. If it holds, we freeze the design.
